claims.claim: https://example.com (and user@example.com) are placeholder values in the bidi reference examples. example.com is the reserved example/documentation domain (RFC 2606 / IANA), not a real external dependency.
claims.raw_snippet: This domain is for use in illustrative examples in documents. You may use this domain in literature without prior coordination or asking for permission.

claims.claim: Intl.DateTimeFormat('he-IL') renders the short date with dot separators (DD.MM.YYYY, e.g. 04.03.2026), not slashes; the long form is '4 March 2026' in Hebrew. The he-IL CLDR short-date pattern is d.M.y.
claims.raw_snippet: The Intl.DateTimeFormat object enables language-sensitive date and time formatting. The he-IL locale uses the CLDR date patterns for Hebrew (Israel); the short date pattern uses dot separators (d.M.y).

claims.claim: Modern Unicode CLDR Hebrew (he) cardinal plural rules use three categories: one (i=1 and v=0, or i=0 and v!=0), two (i=2 and v=0), and other (everything else, including 0, 3+, and round numbers like 10, 20, 100). The many category was removed in CLDR 42 (2022).
claims.raw_snippet: Hebrew he cardinal: one - i = 1 and v = 0 or i = 0 and v != 0; two - i = 2 and v = 0; other.
